title: magnistipula butayei subsp. montana
intro: Magnistipula butayei subsp. montana, also known as the Philippine Stinging Tree, is a subspecies of plant in the family Magnciphyllaceae. It is native to the Philippines and is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating pain and inflammation. The plant contains compounds that exhibit analgesic and anti-inflammatory activities, which can provide relief from headaches, fever, and arthritis. Herbal preparations of Magnistipula butayei subsp. montana include infusions and decoctions, which can be used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and skin infections.
